summary: A coined-term lexicon for modern foreign-affairs discourse.
final_use_case: It matters because IR language can become too vague to do analytic work; this project shows how a term earns use through definition, diagnostic power, adoption logic, and essay/briefing application.

instruments.instrument_name: IR Lexicon — coined-term lexicon for modern foreign-affairs discourse Core Instrument Panel
instruments.input: tool buttons, rooms, workflow steps, analytical controls, and review surfaces
instruments.operation: It moves 400 raw coinages through a lacuna atlas, adoptability shortlist, 20-term canon, 10 mini-theories, 16-dimension adoption scoring, collision-risk flags, and a 25-rule propagation doctrine. Terms such as Credibility Underwriting Gap, Compellence Overdraft, Ambiguity Rent, Sanction Saturation, Standards Entrenchment, Compliance Chokepoints, and Doctrine Lock-In are treated as analytic instruments rather than slogans.
instruments.output: named instruments that perform the useful work rather than generic navigation
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires tool buttons, rooms, workflow steps, analytical controls, and review surfaces
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds named instruments that perform the useful work rather than generic navigation; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: named instruments that perform the useful work rather than generic navigation
instruments.status: Status: The lexicon is complete in the senses that matter for use: the canonical 20 are named with full schema, the lacuna atlas is filled, ten mini-theories.
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept tool buttons, rooms, workflow steps, analytical controls, and review surfaces and produce named instruments that perform the useful work rather than generic.
instruments.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (tool buttons, rooms, workflow steps, analytical...) produces output (named instruments that perform the useful work rather...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

instruments.instrument_name: IR Lexicon — coined-term lexicon for modern foreign-affairs discourse Workflow Orchestration
instruments.input: upstream inputs, intermediate decisions, downstream products, and stale-state conditions
instruments.operation: Publish the canonical 20 in real briefing and essay work, exercise the propagation doctrine against actual writing, and let usage drive which of the remaining ten earn promotion to the mini-theory layer. Two enhancements are deferred and stay deferred until the usage data warrants them. Step 01 Canonical-20 in real writing Lexicon published in real briefing and essay work — the term earns its way in by being used. Step 02 Propagation doctrine exercise 25-rule propagation doctrine exercised against actual writing.

instruments.operation: uses `Vaguer Language Than The Phenomenon Deserves` as a named project component with a traceable role in the public mechanism. Source context: A coined-term lexicon for modern foreign-affairs discourse. The premise: contemporary IR discussion handles many phenomena with vaguer language than the phenomenon deserves — ?credibility,? ?sanctions fatigue,? ?gray zone,? ?standards warfare? — and the work of conceptual sharpening is to coin the term that names the phenomenon precisely.
